input.content:
# Fix off-by-one bug in `processItems`

## Context

Users report that the last item in an array is never processed by `processItems` in `src/utils.ts`. The root cause is an off-by-one error in the loop condition on **line 7**: `i < items.length - 1` stops the loop one iteration too early, skipping the final element.

## The Bug

```typescript
// Line 7 ‚Äî current (buggy)
for (let i = 0; i < items.length - 1; i++) {
```

For an array like `["hello", "world", "foo"]` (length 3), the loop runs for `i = 0, 1` only, never reaching index 2.

## Fix

**File:** `src/utils.ts`, line 7

Change the loop condition from `i < items.length - 1` to `i < items.length`:

```typescript
// Line 7 ‚Äî fixed
for (let i = 0; i < items.length; i++) {
```

This is a one-character change (remove `- 1`).

## Verification

Run the test suite:

```bash
npm test
```

All 6 tests in `tests/utils.test.ts` should pass, including:
- `"should process all items in the array"` ‚Äî `["hello","world","foo"]` ‚Üí `["HELLO","WORLD","FOO"]`
- `"should handle a single item"` ‚Äî `["test"]` ‚Üí `["TEST"]`
- `"should handle two items"` ‚Äî `["a","b"]` ‚Üí `["A","B"]`
- `"should return empty array for empty input"` ‚Äî `[]` ‚Üí `[]`

The first three tests are currently failing due to this bug.

## Files to modify

- `src/utils.ts` ‚Äî line 7 (the only change)

**Bug:** Classic off-by-one error on line 7 of `src/utils.ts`. The loop condition `i < items.length - 1` stops one iteration early, so the last array element is never processed.

**Fix:** Remove `- 1` from the loop condition ‚Üí `i < items.length`. This is a single-line change.
